description: The Crisis is an active American publication and the official magazine of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP). Founded in 1910 by W. E. B. Du Bois (editor), Oswald Garrison Villard, J. Max Barber, Charles Edward Russell, Kelly Miller, William Stanley Braithwaite, and Mary Dunlop Maclean, The Crisis has been in continuous print since 1910, and is the oldest Black-oriented magazine in the world. At present, The Crisis is published quarterly and focuses on civil rights issues, history, politics and culture, and seeks to challenge its readers about issues that continue to plague African Americans and other communities of color.   The magazine's name, suggested by one of the NAACP co-founders and noted white abolitionist, Mary White Ovington, was inspired by James Russell Lowell's 1844 poem, The Present Crisis . Between 1997 and 2003, the magazine appeared as The New Crisis: The Magazine of Opportunities and Ideas , but has since reverted back to The Crisis. Today, the magazine largely operates online via social media outlets on Facebook, Instagram, and through their website, which focuses on current injustices and shares articles from other news outlets.   The Internet Archive Collection contains microfilm published between 1910 and 2014.   The Crisis Magazine can be found online at https://www.thecrisismagazine.com/   Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Crisis   The ISSN is 1559-1573
